other_observers: None.; Christian Nunes found this bird. Several other people saw it. I met Christian on the way to see the bird, and he told me it was still present.
specific_location: Along South Boulder Creek Trail, west of Hwy 93 about a mile south of the edge of Boulder City.
description: Medium sized bird with a very long, forked tail. The head, upper back and throat were gray, with a dark eye and bill. The lower body was light colored, tinged with salmon pink. The wings were black with extensive white fringes to the feathers. The tail was also black with extensive white markings. When the bird flew, it revealed much brighter red markings between the forewing and the body, with much of the underwing a light pink.Behaviors: The bird perched on the available fence lines and on the top of plants a few feet high. It would then fly off on a sally to chase an insect. If it caught one, or not, it would fly back again to a prominent perch, either to eat the insect or to look for another to chase.Call: None heard.Plumage: Alternate
